summary: Iteration 3 — AI Assistant (Asistan) chat + Daily Cases + Activity Notes + mobile shell. Added 23 new pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_chat_cases.py covering daily-cases CRUD + filters + isolation, activity-notes CRUD + isolation, auth enforcement on 8 new endpoints, chat greeting/empty-message, attendance/daily-case/activity-note command execution paths, chat history time-ordering + teacher isolation, chat clear isolation, and voice multipart upload. 22/23 pass. One failure: POST /api/chat/voice returns HTTP 500 when Whisper rejects the payload (e.g., malformed audio) — spec requires graceful 200 + friendly reply. Also re-ran prior suites — all 39 iteration-1/2 tests still pass (62 total, 61 green). Frontend E2E: mobile (400x860) top bar centered 'Öğretmen Çantası', no hamburger, 5-item bottom nav (Panel/Öğrenci/Yoklama/Vakalar/Etkinlik), FAB visible and opens full-screen chat-panel-mobile. Desktop (1440x900) bottom nav hidden, FAB docks chat-panel-desktop at 380×560. Chat send/receive: user bubble + assistant bubble rendered; assistant replied 'Merhaba! Yoklama, günlük vaka veya etkinlik notu için söyleyin.' confirming LLM budget is NOT exhausted. 'Temizle' clears history with toast 'Sohbet temizlendi.'. Daily-cases form create + delete verified. Activity-notes form create + delete verified. Route protection verified (both /daily-cases and /activity-notes redirect to /login without session).

minor.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error when Whisper transcription fails (invalid audio format, budget exhaustion, network errors). Spec requires graceful 200 + friendly reply. Root cause: `stt.transcribe` call on line ~1141 of server.py is NOT wrapped in try/except; the LLM call in _run_assistant has budget-exhaust handling but the Whisper call does not. Fix: wrap the stt.transcribe block (and/or the whole handler after file read) in try/except, and on Exception return {'transcript':'', 'reply':'Sesi anlayamadım veya bağlantı hatası oluştu.', 'executed':[]} with status 200. Check 'budget' keyword for friendly message as done in LLM path.

ui_bugs.issue: Submit button is NOT disabled when activity_name is empty. Spec says 'activity name required'. Likely relies on HTML `required` attribute only; better UX would be to disable `activity-submit-btn` until input has value.

Mic button className did not change when clicked to start recording in headless browser (MediaRecorder may be silently failing due to missing permissions in test env). Cannot confirm if state toggle works with real microphone access. Recommend visual confirmation or forcing recording flag in UI state regardless of MediaRecorder success.

action_items: Wrap `stt.transcribe(...)` in /api/chat/voice with try/except and return 200 + friendly Turkish reply on any exception (matches existing LLM budget-exhaust pattern)
Optional: disable [data-testid='activity-submit-btn'] when activity-name-input is empty for stronger client-side validation
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py now ~1190 lines — STRONG recommendation to split into routers (auth.py, students.py, attendance.py, cases.py, activities.py, chat.py, dashboard.py). Growing past maintainability threshold.

/api/chat/voice error handling asymmetry: _run_assistant catches LLM exceptions with budget-keyword detection but stt.transcribe has NO wrapper. Single point of failure — any Whisper 4xx/5xx bubbles up as 500.
_execute_commands silently drops invalid entries (no student match, bad status enum). Consider echoing 'skipped' info back to the client so the assistant reply can inform the user.

_run_assistant replays prior user turns by calling chat.send_message in a loop — this generates intermediate LLM responses that are discarded, wasting Universal Key budget. Consider using chat history passing API if emergentintegrations supports it, or maintaining a single session_id per teacher to rely on provider-side memory instead of replay.
LLM temperature/max_tokens not set → default values. For a strict JSON-only assistant, setting temperature=0 and max_tokens low would reduce hallucinations and cost.

mark_all_present overwrites explicit entries if it appears AFTER mark_attendance in commands list (both upsert on same key). Enforcing order ('mark_all_present first, then mark_attendance corrections') is delegated to the LLM prompt — brittle. Consider post-processing: execute mark_all_present first regardless of order in the commands array.
All new endpoints properly project _id:0 — verified. Good.
Auth enforcement verified on all 8 new endpoints (daily-cases×2, activity-notes×2, chat/message, chat/history×2, chat/voice) — all return 401 without token.
Teacher isolation verified on daily-cases, activity-notes, and chat_history. Good.
test_credentials: Transient TEST_ui_* user+session injected via mongosh per /app/memory/test_credentials.md for UI run; cleaned up post-run. Backend pytest fixtures (teacher_a, teacher_b) from conftest.py reused.
seed_data_creation: Pytest fixtures create TEST_ prefixed students (TESTAda/TESTCan/TESTDeniz) + cleanup via mongo_db teardown. UI seed: 2 students + 1 user/session, purged after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration-3 backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_chat_cases.py. Run full regression: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. LLM is WORKING (confirmed real Turkish reply received from Claude). Voice endpoint needs try/except wrapper — after fix, update TestChat::test_voice_endpoint_accepts_multipart assertion to strict (200 only) with reply field present. data-testids used: chat-fab, chat-panel-mobile, chat-panel-desktop, chat-input, chat-send-btn, chat-mic-btn, chat-close-btn, chat-clear-btn, msg-user, msg-assistant, bottom-nav, bnav-*, case-{student-select,title-input,desc-input,date-input,submit-btn,row-<id>,delete-<id>}, activity-{name-input,desc-input,date-input,submit-btn,row-<id>,delete-<id>}. NOTE: When selecting chat-input/chat-send-btn on desktop, scope to [data-testid='chat-panel-desktop'] because both mobile+desktop panels are rendered simultaneously (only one visible per viewport).
rca of the issue: POST /api/chat/voice 500 RCA: server.py lines ~1124–1153 define chat_voice handler. The OpenAISpeechToText.transcribe() call (line ~1141) may raise exceptions for: (a) invalid audio format/corrupt payload, (b) LLM/Whisper API budget exhaustion, (c) network errors, (d) unsupported language. Unlike /api/chat/message where _run_assistant catches LLM exceptions and returns a friendly JSON reply, chat_voice has no outer try/except wrapping stt.transcribe; the exception propagates and FastAPI returns 500 Internal Server Error with no payload. Reproduction: POST multipart with any non-audio bytes (tested with 12-byte 'RIFF...WEBM' string) → 500. Mitigation: wrap transcription (and optionally the whole post-read block) in try/except Exception as exc; inspect str(exc) for 'budget' keyword for the same friendly Turkish reply used in _run_assistant, else return generic 'Sesi işleyemedim' — all with 200 status. This aligns with review_request spec: 'handles budget-exceeded errors gracefully with 200 + friendly reply'.
